kpis.label: Target GP by Dec 2027
kpis.note: Set by the Elitez Revenue Engine plan. Measured as external-client GP only — internal retainer GP not counted.

kpis.label: Elitez HQ internal retainer
kpis.note: Baseline funding. Locks the cost floor regardless of external pipeline.

kpis.label: Monthly cost floor
kpis.note: Total unit OpEx. Internal retainer + AI tooling + delivery. External revenue above this is pure upside.

kpis.label: External sweet spot
kpis.note: Core tier ($1,499–$2,499). Most SMEs land here after the ROI Diagnostic.

kpis.label: 1.5-year revenue target
kpis.note: Path to $100K GP by Dec 2027. Assumes mixed Entry/Core/Premium ladder + 2 retainers acquired per month from month 4.

kpis.label: External-client break-even
kpis.note: Benchmark cost per lead on Core tier. Below this, client is net-positive by month 2.

mentalModels.title: The $10K ROI Question
mentalModels.body: SMEs don't buy 'marketing.' They buy ROI. Their core buying question: 'If I give you $X, can I make $10K+ back?' Every pitch must answer this with concrete break-even maths, not vanity metrics.
mentalModels.rule: Never quote a retainer without also quoting break-even deals/mo based on the prospect's ACV.

mentalModels.title: Position Above Risk
mentalModels.body: The trap is selling on 'we're cheaper' — it traps us in direct comparison with $500 freelancers. The move is positioning as the structured, reliable, ROI-focused alternative: above freelancers on reliability, peer of micro-agencies on structure, superior on outcome focus.
mentalModels.rule: If the conversation becomes price-first, redirect to reliability + monthly ROI report.

mentalModels.title: Unit of Sale = Outcome, Not Line Item
mentalModels.body: We sell 'bundled lead-generation systems', not $500 videos and $50 graphics. Change the unit of sale and we escape the freelancer race-to-the-bottom comparison entirely.
mentalModels.rule: Never itemise deliverables in the first email. Quote the system + one headline metric (leads/mo or CPL).

mentalModels.title: The Messy Middle
mentalModels.body: Target SMEs with 10–100 employees — too big for freelancer risk, too small for premium agency budgets. The $799–$2,999 tier is a structured + reliable gap in the market.
mentalModels.rule: If headcount <10 AND deal size <$1K AND spend <$500, recommend a freelancer instead. Protects our case-study quality.

mentalModels.title: Ops-Stack Arbitrage Engine
mentalModels.body: SG sales node closes deals at SG-premium prices. AI layer collapses delivery hours. Elitez Group's 350-person backbone absorbs overhead that micro-agencies load onto retainers (rent, senior-AM tax, finance). Margin = SG price − AI-reduced hours − group-shared overhead.
mentalModels.rule: Never frame the cost saving as 'we're cheap because of geography.' Frame it as structurally lean — AI + group-backed infrastructure — never rate arbitrage.

objections.trigger: A freelancer costs me $500. You're 3× that.
objections.response: That's true for one deliverable. The freelancer version of what's inside Core — 20 posts, 4 videos, SEO, design, website updates — is 4 freelancers × $500 = $2K, plus your time managing them. At $1,499 Core is actually cheaper than that — with one invoice, one accountable owner, and a monthly ROI report.
objections.killshot: Reframe: 'You're not comparing us to one freelancer. You're comparing us to 4 freelancers plus your project-management hours.'

objections.trigger: We already have an in-house person doing this.
objections.response: Good — we don't replace them; we extend them. Pulse becomes their content factory so they can focus on strategy and stakeholder mgmt. Most in-house marketers are drowning in production; we remove that layer.
objections.killshot: Ask: 'What % of their week is spent on content production vs. actual strategy?' The answer is usually 70/30 production-heavy.

objections.trigger: 30-day cancellation sounds too good. What's the catch?
objections.response: No catch. Our retention model is: we ship well, you stay. We'd rather lose a wrong-fit client in month 2 than drag them to month 12 unhappy. Every client who leaves becomes a damaging reference.
objections.killshot: Name it: 'Short-notice cancellation is our accountability mechanism. If we can't prove ROI monthly, you leave — that's the deal.'

objections.trigger: What if you disappear like my last agency?
objections.response: Pulse invoices come from Elitez Pte Ltd — 15-year SG entity, 350+ staff, 90+ active client operations. We're not a solo freelancer ghosting risk; we're a revenue unit of an established group.
objections.killshot: Drop the number: 'Elitez Group has served 90+ ongoing client operations for 15 years. Our disappearing is a group-level event.'

objections.trigger: Can you work on a performance/commission-only model?
objections.response: No. Performance-only models break delivery consistency — when the variable is our income, we optimise for quick wins, not the system you need. Retainer + monthly ROI report keeps our incentives aligned.
objections.killshot: Honest line: 'Performance-only models turn us into freelancers. You don't want that.'

objections.trigger: $1.5K × 12 = $18K. That's a lot for an SME.
objections.response: Flip it: 47 leads/mo × 12 = 564 qualified leads/yr. At $42 CPL, that's benchmark. If your avg deal is $5K and you close 5%, that's 28 new customers × $5K = $140K revenue. $18K retainer → $122K net. That's the maths to have.
objections.killshot: Quote the math: '$18K in, ~$140K pipeline. Worst case 3×; median 7×.' Adjust deal size to their ACV.

objections.trigger: We're too small for you, right?
objections.response: If you're <10 staff with a deal size <$1K, honestly yes — we'd recommend a $500 freelancer. Come back when your ACV or team grows. We'd rather say no than over-promise.
objections.killshot: Be direct and refuse: 'You're not a fit today. Here's a freelancer we trust: [name]. Diagnostic will confirm.'

objections.trigger: We tried agencies before. Didn't work.
objections.response: Valid. The previous model was: big retainer, quarterly deck, no transparency. Ours is the opposite: monthly ROI report on one page, 30-day notice, SG-led + AI-augmented + group-backed overhead so your money doesn't fund senior-AM tax or agency rent.
objections.killshot: Ask: 'What specifically went wrong? Was it reporting, responsiveness, or results?' Then map Pulse to their exact failure mode.

talkTracks.say: Before we talk packages, I want to sketch your ROI break-even live. Tell me your average contract value and typical close rate — I'll run the math in 60 seconds.
Published on the site — Entry is $799, Core is $1,499, Premium $2,999. Which tier fits depends on your numbers. Want to run the 2-min diagnostic together now?

talkTracks.situation: Prospect compares to a specific competitor
talkTracks.say: Fair comparison. [Competitor] is strong at [X]. Where we beat them for an SME your size is [Y] — let me show you the side-by-side.
Let's try 90 days on Core. If the month-2 ROI report doesn't make the maths obvious, you give 30 days' notice and we part friends. No lock-in means the decision is small.
